pattern: Numbers made entirely of set bits are exactly the 'one less than a power of two' family, so the answer is just the first member of that short, easily-enumerated family that reaches n.
hints: A number whose binary form is all 1s is always exactly one less than some power of two.
Starting from the smallest such number, repeatedly double it and add one until it is no longer smaller than n.
There's no need to search bit by bit -- the sequence 1, 3, 7, 15, 31, ... grows fast enough that you'll reach or pass n within about as many steps as n has bits.

edge_inputs.description: statement example three, n already all-ones
n one more than an all-ones number
n exactly a power of two
